title: Alsine rosanoi
taxonomy: Alsine rosanoi is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family of flowering plants. This family consists of approximately 437 genera and 2,430 species, with members found on every continent except Antarctica. Alsine rosanoi belongs to the genus Alsine, which comprises several species of creeping or annual plants. The genus Alsine is often divided into different sections based on morphological characteristics, with Alsine rosanoi falling within one such section. Further classification of Alsine rosanoi would require more detailed analysis of its genetic makeup and specific morphological features.
uses: Alsine rosanoi, also known as Roseroot or Mountain Avens, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Some studies suggest that Alsine rosanoi may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that could help protect against cell damage and promote wound healing.
